definitions: A risk or danger that is an inherent part of a particular job or profession.
A problem or difficulty that is a natural and unavoidable consequence of a particular activity or situation.
examples: Back pain is an occupational hazard for many construction workers.
Dealing with difficult customers is an occupational hazard in retail.
For a travel writer, jet lag is an occupational hazard.
Public speaking can be an occupational hazard for politicians.
Stress is an occupational hazard in high-pressure jobs.
